title: Canadian Policyholder Portal

product_overview.what_is_being_built: A self-service portal for Canadian auto insurance policyholders to manage their accounts and claims.
product_overview.who_it_is_for: The primary users are Canadian auto insurance policyholders, with a focus on providing an accessible and secure experience.
product_overview.value_proposition: The portal will allow policyholders to easily access their policy details, submit claims, and manage their account information, improving customer satisfaction and reducing operational costs.

business_goals: Improve customer satisfaction and retention for Canadian auto insurance policyholders
Reduce call center volume and operational costs by enabling self-service capabilities
user_goals: Easily access policy details and download proof-of-insurance documents
Quickly submit first notice of loss (FNOL) for claims and upload supporting documents
Manage personal information and account settings securely
kpis: Increase in self-service portal utilization rate

in_scope: Personal information management (address, phone, driver's license)
Password reset and self-service account management

high_level_layout: Policy dashboard with details and documents

functional_requirements.description: Secure user authentication and account management

user_actions.user_action: Sign in with email and password

user_actions.system_behavior: Send password reset email to registered email address
Prompt user to set up MFA (e.g., SMS, authenticator app)

fields.name: MFA_Method
fields.description: Multi-factor authentication method (e.g., SMS, authenticator app)

validations: Email must be a valid, registered email address
Password must meet minimum security requirements (length, complexity)
MFA method must be a supported, secure option

functional_requirements.description: View and manage policyholder's insurance policy details

user_actions.system_behavior: Display policy coverage, limits, deductibles, and other relevant information
Generate and provide a PDF version of the policyholder's proof-of-insurance card

fields.name: PolicyNumber
fields.description: Unique identifier for the policyholder's insurance policy

fields.name: CoverageType
fields.description: Type of insurance coverage (e.g., auto, home, life)

fields.name: CoverageLimit
fields.description: Maximum amount the insurance policy will pay out

fields.name: Deductible
fields.description: Amount the policyholder must pay before the insurance coverage begins

validations: PolicyNumber must be a valid, registered policy number
CoverageType must be a supported insurance product
CoverageLimit and Deductible must be positive numeric values

functional_requirements.description: Allow policyholders to submit first notice of loss (FNOL) for claims

user_actions.system_behavior: Capture claim details, generate a unique claim number, and confirm submission
Allow upload of images, PDFs, and other files related to the claim

fields.name: IncidentDate
fields.description: Date the incident occurred that led to the claim

fields.name: IncidentDescription
fields.description: Description of the incident and damages

fields.name: SupportingDocuments
fields.description: Files (images, PDFs) related to the claim

fields.name: ClaimNumber
fields.description: System-generated unique identifier for the insurance claim (generated upon submission)

validations: IncidentDate must be a valid date in the past or today
IncidentLocation must be a valid address or location
IncidentDescription must be a non-empty string
SupportingDocuments must be valid image, PDF, or other supported file types
ClaimNumber is generated by the system and must be unique

functional_requirements.description: Allow policyholders to manage their personal information

user_actions.system_behavior: Allow editing of address, phone number, and other contact details
Allow editing of driver's license number and other related details

validations: FullName must be a valid legal name
DateOfBirth must be a valid date in the past
Address must be a valid residential address
PhoneNumber must be a valid phone number
DriversLicenseNumber must be a valid, registered driver's license number

business_rules: Policyholders must be able to access and manage their own insurance policy details
Policyholders must be able to submit first notice of loss (FNOL) for claims
The system must generate a unique claim number when a new claim is submitted
Policyholders must be able to upload supporting documents for their claims
Policyholders must be able to manage their personal information, including contact details and driver's license information

requirements: The application must meet WCAG AA accessibility standards, including support for keyboard navigation and screen readers
Error messages and form validations must be accessible and provide clear guidance to users
The application must support multi-factor authentication (MFA) for user sign-in
User sessions must be automatically timed out after a period of inactivity
All data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Audit logging must be implemented for security-related events (e.g., sign-in, password reset)
The application must be responsive and load quickly, with a target page load time of less than 2 seconds
The application must be able to handle at least 1,000 concurrent user sessions without degradation in performance
The application must be available 24/7, with a target uptime of at least 99.9%
The application must have a robust disaster recovery plan to ensure business continuity in the event of a major outage

end_to_end_workflows.name: User Authentication
steps: User navigates to the login page
System validates credentials and initiates user session
User is redirected to the dashboard

end_to_end_workflows.name: File a Claim
steps: User navigates to the claims submission page
User provides incident details (date, location, description)
User uploads supporting documents (photos, police report)
System generates a unique claim number
System confirms submission and displays the generated claim number to the user

end_to_end_workflows.name: Update Personal Information
steps: User navigates to the personal information management page
User updates their contact information (address, phone number)
User updates their driver's license information
System validates the new information and confirms the updates

assumptions: Policyholders will have a valid email address and password to authenticate
Policyholders will have access to the required documents (e.g., proof-of-insurance, police report) to submit claims
The insurance company's backend systems can provide the necessary policy and claims data to populate the portal
The backend claims system can generate unique claim numbers reliably and atomically
dependencies: Integration with the insurance company's core policy administration and claims management systems
Availability of a secure document storage solution to host uploaded claim documents
Smooth integration with existing claims and CRM processes
Backend service or API to generate unique claim numbers when new claims are initiated
open_questions: What are the specific requirements for the proof-of-insurance document (e.g., layout, branding)?
How will the portal handle cases where policyholders forget their password or need to reset it?
What are the specific data retention and disposal policies for user and claims data?
What is the format and structure of the system-generated claim number (e.g., alphanumeric, prefix, length)?
Should the claim number be immediately visible to the user upon submission, or delivered via email confirmation?
